components.prompt: Read the flow-test workflow definition in Flow/flow-test.Flow.json. Identify all 7 components and their connections. Plan the execution path for: {#prompt#}. Produce a step-by-step trace showing which component IDs will execute and in what order.
Is the Plan mode dialog component (ID 0) correctly executed and understood?

Build: Create a markdown report documenting the flow-test execution. Include: (1) workflow component list, (2) execution trace for --true mode, (3) execution trace for --false mode showing cycle detection. Context: {#prompt#}
Is the Build mode dialog (ID 2) executed and is the report being generated?

Goal: Verify that all 7 components have been visited. Confirm: dialog Plan (ID 0), logic TRUE->2 (ID 1), dialog Build (ID 2), logic TRUE->4 (ID 3), dialog Goal (ID 4), logic TRUE->6 (ID 5), dialog Goal finish (ID 6). Input: {#prompt#}. Mark goal complete when all confirmed.
Has component ID 4 (Goal mode) completed its verification?
Goal: flow-test workflow complete. All 3 dialog modes (Plan, Build, Goal) exercised. All 4 logic branches tested. {#prompt#} verified. Generate the final execution summary.
